channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Almost indestructible memory card launched; Lexar's Armor SD steel cards are water resistant and can survive a 16-feet drop
item.description: Lexar stainless-steel SD cards are 37 times stronger than standard ones, offering durability for even extreme conditions.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The adorable BDX Droids are coming to more Disney Parks around the world, including Disney World
item.description: Disney Parks is bringing the adorable and impressive 'BDX Droids' to more parks worldwide, including Disney World. Plus, more details were unveiled for the update on the Smugglers Run attraction at Galaxy's Edge.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: iPad shoppers beware: One of the new models is not like the others
item.description: This week, Apple updated half of its iPad lineup. After updating the iPad Pro and iPad mini in 2024, the company has just unveiled a third-generation iPad Air and an eleventh-generation iPad.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Alibaba doubles down on RISC-V architecture with a new secretive 'server-grade' chip that will put AMD and Intel on alert
item.description: Alibaba’s research arm has launched its first server-grade RISC-V processor to take on Intel and AMD.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: SpaceX’s Starship explodes again, with wreckage seen from Florida
item.description: Nearly two months after an explosion sent flaming debris raining down on the Turks and Caicos, SpaceX launched another mammoth Starship rocket on Thursday, but lost contact minutes into the test flight as the spacecraft came tumbling down and broke apart.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: February’s IT job market in the US shows mixed signals; unemployment ticks up
item.description: “Sluggish” might be the best word to describe the IT job market after the US Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) published its data for February employment today. The data offered mixed signals for US tech jobs: even though the number of tech roles grew, unemployment rose.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Scientists date remains of an ancient child that resembles both humans and Neanderthals
item.description: Scientists have dated the skeleton of an ancient child that caused a stir when it was first discovered because it carries features from both humans and Neanderthals.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Out of the lab and into the streets, researchers and doctors rally for science against Trump cuts
item.description: Researchers, doctors, their patients and supporters are venturing out of labs, hospitals and offices across the country to stand up to what they call an attack on life-saving science by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NordVPN reacts to results from its latest security audit
item.description: The independent security check conucted by Cure53 comes only a few weeks after a successful no-log audit. Here's all you need to know.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Logitech says San Jose fuels big edge for talent, tech and connections
item.description: Logitech believes its quest to ride the artificial intelligence boom is getting a huge boost because of its San Jose location.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Thousands of WordPress sites targeted with malicious plugin backdoor attacks
item.description: Four separate backdoors deployed on vulnerable websites, allowing for persistent access, remote code execution, and more.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Private lunar lander is declared dead after landing sideways in a crater near the moon's south pole
item.description: A private lunar lander is no longer working after landing sideways in a crater near the moon's south pole.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Anthropic’s upgraded Console targets more collaboration among developers
item.description: Anthropic has upgraded its Console — an interface that lets enterprises create API keys, add more users to a team, set up billing, and experiment with Claude on the Workbench, in an effort to promote more collaboration.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: i2CAT promueve el primer laboratorio abierto europeo de investigación 6G en órbita baja
item.description: El centro de investigación i2CAT ha anunciado en el marco del Mobile World Congress la puesta en marcha del primer laboratorio en órbita terrestre baja de Europa (LEO, por sus siglas en inglés).
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 8 obstacles women still face when seeking a leadership role in IT
item.description: If you are a tech leader, you might encourage your daughter to follow your path, imagining a journey, like yours, with challenges that can be overcome with hard work. But if you are a man — especially a white man — you are likely unaware of the massive obstacles she will face that you didn’t.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The Gig Economy's Future: Flex Endorses Trump's Labor Department Nominees
item.description: Flex, a lobbying group representing America's largest gig companies, endorses President Trump's nominees to lead the Department of Labor. The endorsement could shape the future of worker classification in the gig economy.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Albania shuts down TikTok for a year amid concerns over violence among children
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — The Albanian Cabinet decided on Thursday to shut down TikTok for 12 months, blaming the popular video-sharing platform for inciting violence and bullying, especially among children.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: HPE cuts 2,500 jobs, expects Juniper buy to close year-end ’25, faces tariff issues
item.description: Despite a mostly positive revenue report for the first quarter of its fiscal 2025, HPE executives detailed a number of challenges the company will face in the coming months, including layoffs, a court case over its proposed buy of Juniper Networks, and the U.S. government’s tariff plan.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Siemens announces $285 million investment in US manufacturing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Siemens plans to invest $285 million in manufacturing in the United States, the tech company said Thursday, including two new facilities in California and Texas. The U.S. is the largest market for the company. The recent investments in the Siemens' U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Salesforce’s AI agents can decode tariffs in minutes
item.description: Tariffs have caused lots of headaches for business owners around the world, especially as they’ve become a hot topic in the political landscape. Just this week, President Donald Trump enacted 25% import taxes on Mexican and Canadian goods, causing the S&P 500 to plummet.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah on Wednesday became the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices. The bill headed to the desk of Gov.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Walk through your future home or business space before construction starts
item.description: Lifesize Plans offers immersive walk-throughs that allow customers the opportunity to tour a home's design before construction actually begins.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft launches genAI sales agents, also focuses on finance and supply chain
item.description: Microsoft is planning a string of new AI agents to automate mundane tasks and improve worker productivity. The company on Wednesday announced two AI-based sales agents that can autonomously follow up on sales leads.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Judge denies Elon Musk's request to block OpenAI for-profit conversion but welcomes trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — A federal judge has denied Elon Musk's request for a court order blocking OpenAI from converting itself to a for-profit company but said she could expedite a trial to consider Musk's claims against the ChatGPT maker and its CEO. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: UK watchdog drops competition review of Microsoft's OpenAI deal
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain's competition watchdog said Wednesday it has dropped its review of Microsoft's partnership with OpenAI, saying it's satisfied that the deal doesn't need a closer investigation under the country's merger rules.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why you should use dependency injection
item.description: A few weeks ago, I wrote about how a good software team will defer decisions as long as possible. Smart teams will design and build systems that don’t lock them into any particular implementation until it is utterly necessary.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Georgia lawmakers push to ban automated speed cameras near schools
item.description: DECATUR, Ga. (AP) — Outside Beacon Hill Middle School in the Atlanta suburb of Decatur, like along hundreds of roadsides across Georgia, the unblinking eye of a camera tickets drivers who speed through a school zone.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Authorities use high tech to enhance border security
item.description: Surveillance cameras and drones give border officials an advantage; but the challenge of data-sharing silos remains.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: UK data protection watchdog investigating how TikTok uses children's personal data
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K.'s data protection watchdog said Monday that it's investigating how TikTok uses the personal information of teenagers to deliver content recommendations to them when they use the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, ABC 4
